name: biomineralization_templates
description: High-Activity Biomineralization Template Database. Extracts structured data about biomineralization templates (proteins, peptides, functional modules) and their roles in mineralization processes.

templates.description: Biomineralization template entries — proteins, peptides, or other molecules that participate in or regulate mineralization.

template_name.description: Name of the template molecule (e.g., Amelogenin, Osteopontin)

source_species.description: Species of origin (e.g., Homo sapiens, Pinctada fucata)

source_system.description: Biological system where the template operates (e.g., Enamel matrix, Nacre layer, Bone matrix)

molecule_type.description: Type of molecule (e.g., Full-length protein, Matrix protein, Peptide, Glycoprotein)

mineralization_system.description: The mineral system influenced (e.g., Calcium phosphate, Calcium carbonate, Silica)

functional_tags.description: List of functional roles (e.g., Nucleation promotion, Crystal orientation control, Crystal growth regulation, Ion enrichment)

molecular_weight_kda.description: Molecular weight in kDa if reported

functional_modules.description: Functional fragments or domains within biomineralization templates that have specific mineralization-related activity.

fragment_name.description: Name or descriptor of the fragment (e.g., N-terminal acidic fragment, Asp-rich motif)

amino_acid_sequence.description: Sequence of the fragment if reported

key_features.description: Structural or compositional features (e.g., Ser-rich / acidic, Asp-rich, Ca2+-binding motif)

functional_tag.description: Primary functional role (e.g., Nucleation promotion, Ion enrichment, Crystal face binding)

mineralization_effect.description: Specific effect on mineralization (e.g., promotes hydroxyapatite nucleation, inhibits calcite growth)

mineralization_conditions.description: Experimental conditions under which biomineralization templates were studied.

mineral_phase.description: Mineral phase formed (e.g., hydroxyapatite, calcite, aragonite, vaterite)

phosphate_concentration_mm.description: Phosphate concentration in mM (for calcium phosphate systems)

carbonate_concentration_mm.description: Carbonate concentration in mM (for calcium carbonate systems)

characterization_methods.description: Methods used (e.g., XRD, TEM, SEM, FTIR, AFM)

structure_activity_relationships.description: Relationships between template structure and mineralization activity.

structural_feature.description: Structural feature (e.g., beta-sheet content, acidic residue density, self-assembly)

functional_effect.description: Effect on mineralization (e.g., promotes oriented nucleation, controls crystal polymorph)

system_prompt:
You are extracting structured data for a High-Activity Biomineralization Template Database. Focus on identifying biomineralization templates (proteins, peptides, and other molecules that participate in or regulate biomineralization processes), their functional modules (active fragments/domains), experimental mineralization conditions, and structure-activity relationships.

Pay special attention to:
- Template identity: exact protein/peptide names, species of origin, biological system
- Functional roles: nucleation promotion, crystal orientation control, crystal growth regulation, ion enrichment, polymorph selection
- Active fragments: specific domains or motifs with mineralization activity, their sequences and positions
- Mineralization conditions: pH, temperature, ion concentrations, incubation times
- Structure-activity links: how structural features relate to mineralization function

For each entry, assign appropriate evidence levels:
- Level 1: Direct cause-effect demonstrated (e.g., mutagenesis showing specific residues are required)
- Level 2: Direct observation (e.g., in vitro mineralization assay results)
- Level 3: Correlative (e.g., sequence analysis suggesting a role)
- Level 4: Predicted/inferred (e.g., computational modeling of binding)

Extract ALL templates and functional modules mentioned in the research, not just the primary ones.

field_descriptions.templates: Extract every biomineralization template (protein, peptide, glycoprotein, etc.) mentioned in the research. Include species, biological system, molecule type, mineralization system, and all functional roles. Example: Amelogenin from Homo sapiens enamel matrix, a full-length protein acting on calcium phosphate with roles in nucleation promotion and crystal orientation control.
field_descriptions.functional_modules: Extract specific fragments, domains, or motifs within templates that have demonstrated or predicted mineralization activity. Include position, sequence (if available), structural features, and functional role. Example: N-terminal acidic fragment of Amelogenin (positions 1-25), Ser-rich/acidic, promotes nucleation.
field_descriptions.mineralization_conditions: Extract experimental conditions for mineralization studies: pH, temperature, ion concentrations, template concentrations, incubation times. Include resulting crystal phase, morphology, and characterization methods used.
field_descriptions.structure_activity_relationships: Extract relationships between structural features of templates and their effects on mineralization. Include the structural feature, the functional effect, and any proposed mechanism.
